content.problemStatement: Given an English letter as the character chchch, check whether it is a vowel or not. Note that it can be capital or lower case.
constraints: Each character is an English letter (A-Z or a-z)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single English alphabet character (uppercase or lowercase).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "True" if the character is a vowel (a, e, i, o, u) irrespective of case, otherwise output "False".

sampleTestCases.explanation: The character 'A' (case‑insensitive) is a vowel, so the answer is True.
The character 'b' is not a vowel, therefore the answer is False.
The character 'E' is a vowel, so the output is True.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. For each of the T test cases read the character, convert it to lower case and check whether it belongs to the set {'a','e','i','o','u'}. Print "True" if it does, otherwise print "False". The check is O(1) per character.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T characters.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.
